subject: Anthropology
title: Clans and Lineages
description: Learn Clans and Lineages in one minute.

hook.text: A lineage traces every link back to a known ancestor. A clan claims a shared ancestor but can't prove the links.

buildup.text: Lineages are descent groups where every genealogical link is known. Clans share a founding ancestor, often mythical.

discovery.text: Both organize land, marriage rules, and political alliances. They define who you are and where you belong in society.

twist.text: Clan ancestors may be animals, plants, or spirits. The Iroquois Bear clan claims descent from a bear, not a person.

climax.text: Clans and lineages are the backbone of many societies. They organize politics, property, and identity through kinship.

punchline.text: Your ancestor defines your group, real or mythic.

quiz.question: How do clans differ from lineages?
options: Clans claim a shared ancestor but can't prove every genealogical link
Clans are always smaller than lineages
